parts.PROVOZOVÁNÍ MEZINÁRODNÍ SILNIČNÍ DOPRAVY: OPERATION OF INTERNATIONAL ROAD TRANSPORT AND OPERATION OF ROAD TRANSPORT IN THE TERRITORY OF THE CZECH REPUBLIC BY FOREIGN OPERATORS
parts.PROVOZOVÁNÍ SILNIČNÍ DOPRAVY PRO CIZÍ PO: OPERATION OF ROAD TRANSPORT FOR HIRE OR REWARD
parts.PŘEPRAVA NEBEZPEČNÝCH VĚCÍ V SILNIČNÍ DO: CARRIAGE OF DANGEROUS GOODS BY ROAD
parts.STÁTNÍ SPRÁVA A STÁTNÍ ODBORNÝ DOZOR V S: STATE ADMINISTRATION AND STATE TECHNICAL SUPERVISION IN ROAD TRANSPORT

p1.heading: Subject matter of regulation
p1.text: This Act regulates the conditions for the operation of road transport, as well as the rights and obligations of legal and natural persons associated therewith, and the powers and scope of authority of state administration bodies in this field.

p2.heading: Basic concepts
p2.text:
(1) Road transport for own account is transport that ensures business activity for which the person operating road transport is authorized according to special regulations.^1)

(2) Road transport for hire or reward is transport where a legal relationship, based on a contract whose subject is the carriage of persons, animals or goods, arises between the road transport operator and the person whose transport need is satisfied.

(3) Regular passenger transport is the regular provision of transport services on a specified route of a transport path, where passengers alight and board at pre-determined stops. Regular passenger transport may be operated in the form of public regular transport or in the form of special regular transport. For the purposes of this Act, this means

- a) public regular transport means transport where transport services are offered under pre-announced conditions and are provided to satisfy transport needs,

- b) special regular transport means transport for specified selected groups of passengers, excluding other persons.

(4) Taxi service is public road transport that provides immediate and prompt carriage of persons and their luggage by passenger cars.

(5) A road transport operator (hereinafter referred to as „carrier“) is a legal or natural person who operates road transport for hire or reward or for own account according to this Act. A domestic carrier is a natural person with permanent residence or a legal person with its registered office in the Czech Republic, who operates transport with road motor vehicles registered in the Czech Republic. A foreign carrier is a natural person with permanent residence or a legal person with its registered office outside the territory of the Czech Republic, who operates transport with road motor vehicles registered abroad.

(6) International road transport is road transport where the place of origin and the place of destination are located in the territory of two different states, or a circular journey through the territory of at least two different states.

(7) Urban bus transport is public regular passenger transport operated to satisfy the transport needs of a city.

(8) Transport authorities according to this Act are district authorities.^2) For urban bus transport, the transport authority is the municipal office, city office or magistracy.^3)

(9) A tariff in road transport is a price list for individual transport services provided and the conditions for their use.

p3.heading: Carrier's obligations
p3.text:
(1) The carrier is obliged to

- a) use a road motor vehicle (hereinafter referred to as „vehicle“) in road transport, for which the period since its technical inspection has not exceeded one year, and in international road passenger transport, a period not exceeding six months,

- b) ensure, when organizing drivers' work, compliance with driving time, safety breaks, and rest periods stipulated by special regulations.^4)

(2) The carrier is obliged to keep records of the operation of each vehicle and to retain these records for a period of five years from the end of the carriage. The method of keeping vehicle operation records shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(3) The carrier is obliged to ensure that the following documents are present in each vehicle during operation:

- a) vehicle operation record,

- b) record of vehicle driving time and safety breaks,

- c) other documents according to this Act.

p4.text:
(1) Anyone intending to operate road transport for hire or reward must be reliable, professionally competent, and must have

- a) a concession, if the operation of road transport for hire or reward is a trade,^5) or

- b) a permit from the transport authority in other cases.

(2) The condition of reliability and professional competence does not have to be met by an applicant for the operation of road transport for hire or reward if they ensure that it is met by a person appointed by them and accountable to them.

p5.text: A person who has operated road transport for hire or reward unlawfully during the last three years before submitting the application, and a person whose concession or permit to operate road transport was revoked during the last three years before submitting the application due to violations of obligations imposed by legal regulations related to the operation of road transport, or because they failed to comply with the conditions stipulated in the concession or permit, shall not be considered reliable for the operation of road transport for hire or reward.

p6.text:
(1) Professional competence for the operation of road transport for hire or reward (hereinafter referred to as „professional competence“) in the form of public regular transport, international bus transport, and international freight transport is demonstrated by the applicant with a certificate of a successfully passed examination in subjects stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) The examinations mentioned in paragraph 1 are taken by the applicant before the relevant transport authority at the place of the applicant's registered office or permanent residence.

(3) Professional competence in subjects stipulated by an implementing regulation is substituted by a certificate of a state or matriculation examination in the given field, provided that the curriculum ensures the knowledge required from the perspective of professional competence.

(4) Professional competence, the content and scope of examinations, and details on taking examinations shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p7.heading: Permit procedure
p7.text:
(1) The transport authority decides on a permit for the operation of road transport for hire or reward, which is not a trade under the Trades Licensing Act, according to the location of the applicant's registered office or permanent residence.

(2) The permit is granted based on an application from a person intending to operate road transport for hire or reward.

(3) More detailed conditions for issuing permits for the operation of road transport for hire or reward shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

p8.heading: Opinion on concession decision-making
p8.text:
(1) In the opinion required for the issuance of a concession^5), the transport authority shall state whether the concession applicant meets the condition of reliability, the type of transport for which the opinion is granted, the time scope, and possibly other conditions for the operation of transport.

(2) The authority competent to issue an opinion pursuant to paragraph 1 is

- a) the transport authority at the location of the applicant's registered office or permanent residence, if it concerns a concession for road motor transport,

- b) the transport authority in whose district the transport is to be operated, if it concerns a concession for taxi services; this transport authority shall issue an opinion for its area of jurisdiction and for transport services that begin or end in its territorial district.

p9.heading: Obligations of a road transport entrepreneur
p9.text:
(1) A road transport entrepreneur (hereinafter referred to as “entrepreneur”) is obliged to mark taxi service vehicles, buses, and trucks with a useful load exceeding 3.5 tonnes or a total weight exceeding 6 tonnes used for business with their trade name. The method of marking shall be stipulated by an implementing regulation.

(2) The entrepreneur is further obliged to ensure that

- a) the state of the technical base for road transport corresponds in equipment and scope to the road transport operated by them,

- b) in every vehicle used for business, a document proving the authorization to conduct business (concession deed, license) is present during its operation,

- c) the work of a driver in international freight transport and bus transport is performed only by a person who has reached the age of 21 years and is of good character^5) and, before commencing the performance of driver's duties, has worked for at least two years as a driver of a truck with a total weight exceeding 7.5 tonnes.

p10.text:
(1) A carrier holding a concession or permit for the operation of road transport for hire or reward and intending to operate scheduled passenger transport may commence its operation only on the basis of a license for the operation of scheduled passenger transport (hereinafter referred to as “license”) granted by the transport authority.

(2) The transport authority decides on the license based on the carrier's application. The application for a license must contain:

- a) proof of issuance of the concession or permit,

- b) the form of scheduled passenger transport, including whether it is domestic or international transport,

- c) for a special form of scheduled transport, the category of passengers and information about the legal or natural person for whom this transport is to be operated,

- d) a proposed timetable,

- e) the route of the line, including stops for boarding and alighting and the designation of the initial and final stops (hereinafter referred to as “line routing”),

- f) a proposed tariff,

- g) proposed contractual transport conditions,

- h) daily driving time, continuous driving time, rest period for each driver, safety breaks, if the applicant intends to operate international scheduled passenger transport.

(3) The transport authority in whose territorial district the initial stop will be located is competent to decide on the granting of a license, with the exception of international scheduled passenger transport, where the Ministry of Transport is competent to decide.

p11.text:
(1) The transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, shall submit the application for a license to the transport authorities in whose territorial district the stops will be located (hereinafter referred to as “affected authority”) for an opinion.

(2) The affected authorities are obliged to issue an opinion within 30 days of receiving the application for a license.

(3) Before issuing an opinion, the affected authority shall discuss the application for a license

- a) with the municipality in whose territorial district the stops will be located,

- b) with the competent authority of the Police of the Czech Republic from the perspective of road safety,

- c) with the road administrator,^6)

- d) with the carrier who established the stop sign where, according to the application, a new stop is to be placed.

(4) General regulations on administrative proceedings do not apply to the issuance of an opinion.

p12.text:
(1) The transport authority shall decide on an application for a license for intra-district scheduled passenger transport within 45 days of its submission, and for other domestic scheduled passenger transport within 60 days of its submission. In the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport shall decide on an application for a license within 30 days of receiving permission from the competent authority of the foreign state for the line routing on the territory of that state.

(2) In the public interest of satisfying transport needs, the transport authority or the Ministry of Transport may condition the granting of a license on the carrier's consent to operate another line or a line routing different from the carrier's application.

(3) The transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, shall not grant a license if:

- a) the application for a license has not been approved by the competent authority of the Police of the Czech Republic from the perspective of road safety,

- b) transport needs in the transport connection where the line will be routed are satisfactorily met by the operation of other subsidized passenger transport,

- c) the carrier has not consented to the conditioning of the license granting pursuant to paragraph 2.

(4) The transport authority and the Ministry of Transport may stipulate conditions for the operation of scheduled passenger transport in the decision on granting the license.

p13.text:
In the decision on the license, the transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, shall state:

- a) the form of scheduled passenger transport, including whether it is domestic or international transport; for special scheduled transport, the category of passengers,

- b) the line routing and the names of the stops,

- c) the date of commencement of operation of the scheduled passenger transport,

- d) the period for which the license is granted,

- e) other conditions for the operation of scheduled passenger transport, if decided upon.

p14.text:
(1) In the public interest of satisfying transport needs or in the interest of transport safety, the transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, may initiate proceedings for the amendment of the decision on granting a license.

(2) Within the scope of proceedings for the amendment of a decision pursuant to paragraph 1, the transport authority and the Ministry of Transport may impose on the carrier an extension of conditions or their amendment compared to the previous decision on granting a license.

p15.text:
The transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, shall revoke the license if the license holder:

- a) seriously violates the obligations stipulated by this Act,

- b) fails to fulfill the conditions stipulated in the license,

- c) has not commenced operating scheduled passenger transport based on the granted license,

- d) has requested the revocation of the license.

p16.text:
The license shall cease to be valid:

- a) upon the expiration of the period for which it was granted,

- b) upon the termination of the concession or permit,

- c) by a decision of the transport authority or a decision of the Ministry of Transport, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, on the revocation of the license.

p17.heading: Timetable
p17.text:
(1) A carrier in public scheduled transport shall submit the timetable and its amendment for approval to the transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, to the Ministry of Transport. The transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, shall approve the timetable or its amendment if it meets the requirements stipulated by the implementing regulation. The transport authority competent to decide on the granting of the license shall be competent to approve the timetable or its amendment.

(2) For reasons of public interest, the transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, may decide on an amendment to the timetable even during its validity.

(3) General regulations on administrative proceedings shall not apply to decisions on timetables.

(4) The method of processing, content, and promulgation of the timetable and its amendments shall be stipulated by the implementing regulation.

p18.heading: Obligations of a carrier in public scheduled transport
p18.text:
A carrier in public scheduled transport is obliged to:

- a) commence operation on the line on the date stipulated in the license and maintain the operation of the line for the duration of the license's validity,

- b) issue and publish the timetable, its amendments, contractual transport conditions, and tariff,

- c) operate transport according to the timetable, contractual transport conditions, and tariff,

- d) transport everyone by the connection listed in the timetable, provided that the contractual transport conditions and tariff are met and unless prevented by circumstances that the carrier cannot avert or prevent,

- e) mark the vehicles of the respective line with the name of the destination stop and ensure the identification of the vehicle driver,

- f) establish a stop sign with its name, if the stop has not yet been established, and display the timetable on it; in urban transport, also the line number,

- g) ensure the maintenance of the stop sign, if established by the carrier, or contribute to its maintenance by another establishing entity.

p19.heading: Public service obligations
p19.text:
(1) For the purposes of this Act, „public service obligations“ mean operational, transport, and tariff obligations in scheduled passenger transport in favor of the state or a self-governing territorial unit, which the carrier in scheduled passenger transport is obliged to accept and which it would otherwise not accept in its commercial interest or would accept only partially.

(2) A public service obligation in scheduled passenger transport arises on the basis of a written agreement concluded between the transport authority, the municipality, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, and the carrier.

(3) In case of urgent need in the public interest, a public service obligation may arise on the basis of a decision of the transport authority, or, in the case of international scheduled passenger transport, the Ministry of Transport, even before the conclusion of a written agreement on the public service obligation pursuant to paragraph 2. This decision may be issued only for a definite period, for a maximum of six months. An appeal against this decision shall not have suspensive effect. The transport authority that decides on the granting of the license shall be competent to decide.

(4) The transport authority, municipality, or Ministry of Transport are obliged to compensate the carrier fulfilling the public service obligation for the demonstrable loss incurred as a result of fulfilling this obligation. This loss shall be reimbursed from the budget of the respective district authorities, municipalities, or the Ministry of Transport.

p20.heading: Owners and administrators of land communications
p20.text: The owners and administrators of land communications along the line's route are obliged to tolerate the placement of stop marking equipment only to the absolutely necessary extent. If the owner of the land communication incurs property damage as a result of the placement of stop marking equipment, the carrier in scheduled passenger transport is obliged to pay compensation for the incurred damage.

p21.heading: Taxi service
p21.text:
(1) The taxi service operator is obliged to mark the taxi vehicle with a roof lamp with the inscription „TAXI“ and equip it with an officially verified, properly registering, and sealed taximeter.

(2) The taxi service operator is obliged to ensure that the driver issues the passenger a document of fare payment.

(3) Details on the technical conditions for operating a taxi service and the requirements for the document pursuant to paragraph 2 shall be stipulated by the implementing regulation.

p22.text:
(1) „Dangerous goods“ are substances and articles whose properties (in particular toxicity, corrosivity, flammability, explosiveness, self-ignition, infectiousness, radioactivity) may endanger the safety of persons and property or components of the environment during their transport.

(2) It is permitted to transport by road only dangerous goods defined by an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound and which has been promulgated in the Collection of Laws.^7)

(3) Dangerous goods specified by an implementing regulation, the transport of which may particularly endanger the safety of persons and property or components of the environment, may be transported only on the basis of a permit from the Ministry of Transport; this does not apply to the transport of dangerous goods carried out by the armed forces in the performance of their own tasks.

(4) A permit pursuant to paragraph 3 shall be granted for transport operations for a specific period, for a maximum of one year. In this permit, the Ministry of Transport may specify conditions regarding restrictions on loading or unloading at certain places and at certain times, the route on the transport path, and escort.

(5) Dangerous goods may be submitted for transport and transported only in compliance with special conditions stipulated by special regulations^8) and the implementing regulation.

p23.text:
(1) The consignor is obliged to hand over to the carrier for each consignment of dangerous goods a consignment note in which the dangerous goods are declared. The consignor is obliged to warn the carrier of the dangers arising from the nature of the dangerous goods being transported and, in specified cases, to hand over to the carrier written instructions for an emergency. The implementing regulation shall stipulate the method of declaring dangerous goods in the consignment note, when written instructions for the transport of dangerous goods need to be prepared, including the content of these instructions.

(2) The carrier is obliged to check, according to the documents handed over to him by the consignor, whether the dangerous goods submitted for transport are permitted to be transported by road, and to ensure that the vehicle crew is familiar with the nature of the dangerous goods and with the measures specified in the written instructions. The vehicle crew is obliged to proceed according to the instructions provided.

p24.text:
(1) The carrier is entitled to use for the transport of dangerous goods only vehicles that are suitable for such transport. The conditions for the suitability of vehicles for the transport of dangerous goods are stipulated by a special regulation.

(2) The carrier is obliged to ensure that the transport of dangerous goods is carried out only by drivers who are qualified for such transport. The conditions for the qualification of drivers for the transport of dangerous goods are stipulated by a special regulation.

(3) The carrier is obliged to ensure that the driver, when transporting dangerous goods, has in the vehicle a consignment note for each consignment of dangerous goods separately, a permit for the transport of dangerous goods pursuant to § 22 para. 3, written instructions pursuant to § 23 para. 1, and other documents pursuant to special regulations.^7)^, ^8)

p25.text: In passenger transport, a passenger is entitled to take dangerous goods into the vehicle as luggage only to the extent and under the conditions stipulated by the transport regulations.

p26.text: For the operation of international road transport, the provisions of this Act shall apply, unless an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound and which has been promulgated in the Collection of Laws stipulates otherwise.

p27.text: The provisions on vehicle marking and keeping records of vehicle operation do not apply to foreign carriers. However, foreign carriers and their vehicle crews are obliged to keep records of compliance with driving times, safety breaks, and rest periods.

p28.text: A concession or permit for the operation of international road transport does not replace a decision of the authority of a foreign state through whose territory the transport is to be carried out.

p29.text:
(1) A domestic carrier must have a foreign permit for the operation of international road passenger transport pursuant to an international treaty or the legal order of the foreign state to or through which the transport is carried out.

(2) A domestic carrier shall submit an application for a foreign permit to the Ministry of Transport at least 45 days before the intended transport of persons, or, in the case of scheduled passenger transport, at least seven months before the intended commencement of operation.

p30.text:
(1) If a foreign state, on the basis of an international treaty, hands over to the Czech Republic for domestic carriers a collectively agreed number of non-addressed foreign entry permits for transport, the Ministry of Transport shall decide on the allocation of the foreign entry permit on the basis of an application from the domestic carrier. The Ministry of Transport may entrust a legal entity with the allocation of foreign entry permits.

(2) In the allocated foreign entry permit, the Ministry of Transport or the entrusted legal entity shall indicate the business name of the domestic carrier and the period of validity. The conditions for the allocation of foreign entry permits shall be stipulated by the implementing regulation.

(3) General regulations on administrative proceedings do not apply to the issuance of permits pursuant to paragraphs 1 and 2.

p31.text:
(1) A foreign carrier may carry out road transport to a place in the territory of the Czech Republic and from such a place to a place located outside the territory of the Czech Republic, as well as across the territory of the Czech Republic (in transit), only on the basis of an allocated entry permit of the Czech Republic. The Ministry of Transport decides on entry permits.

(2) An entry permit may be allocated only to a foreign carrier who is authorized to operate international road transport according to the regulations valid in the state in whose territory he has his registered office or residence.

(3) An entry permit is not required for a foreign carrier if it concerns road transport by passenger car or occasional transport in the form of a sightseeing tour or in the form of passenger transport to a specified place.

p32.text:
(1) Unless otherwise provided by an international treaty by which the Czech Republic is bound and which has been promulgated in the Collection of Laws, foreign carriers are authorized to operate road transport between places located in the territory of the Czech Republic only on the basis of a special permit from the Ministry of Transport.

(2) A special permit may be issued only to a foreign carrier who is authorized to operate international road transport according to the regulations valid in the state where it has its registered office or residence.

(3) A special permit is granted for the performance of one transport service or for a limited or unlimited number of services within a certain period, for a maximum of one year.

p33.text: The Ministry of Transport may prohibit a foreign carrier from operating transport in the territory of the Czech Republic for a specified period, not exceeding five years, in the event of a serious or repeated breach of obligations arising from this Act.

p34.text:
(1) State professional supervision in road transport is carried out by transport authorities. In the exercise of state professional supervision, they oversee whether carriers comply with the conditions and fulfill the obligations stipulated by this Act and whether they operate transport according to the granted permit.

(2) A person authorized to perform state professional supervision is entitled, in connection with the exercise of supervision, unless special regulations provide otherwise, to enter establishments and all premises related to the activity performed, to inspect documents and records concerning the operation of road transport. The carrier is obliged to enable the person authorized to perform state professional supervision to exercise the aforementioned authorization. The driver of a vehicle is obliged to present to these persons the documents that must be in the vehicle during its operation according to this Act.

(3) In the performance of state professional supervision, the person authorized to perform state professional supervision is obliged to identify themselves with a document of authorization. The document of authorization must contain the name of the authorized person, the scope of their authorization, and must be confirmed by the authority that issued it.

(4) Supreme state supervision in all matters of road transport is exercised by the Ministry of Transport. Supreme state supervision oversees the performance of state professional supervision carried out by transport authorities. The persons authorized to perform supreme state supervision are subject to the authorizations and obligations under paragraphs 2 and 3 in the exercise of supervision. Transport authorities and carriers are obliged to enable the person authorized to perform supreme state supervision to exercise supervision and, for this purpose, to provide all information and documents about the activities of state professional supervision.

p35.text:
(1) The transport authority or the Ministry of Transport, in case of a violation of this Act, shall impose a fine of up to CZK 200,000 on a carrier who:

- a) fails to comply with the conditions for operating scheduled passenger transport stipulated in the license,

- b) operates road transport without the relevant permit,

- c) fails to ensure compliance with the stipulated driving time and stipulated rest period for drivers,

- d) fails to ensure proper keeping of vehicle operation records,

- e) fails to store vehicle operation records and driver work regime records for the stipulated period,

- f) fails to comply with the conditions for operating taxi services,

- g) marks passenger cars used for providing transport services in a manner interchangeable with taxi vehicles or offers transport services in a manner that is interchangeable with taxi services,

- h) uses vehicles in road transport for which the period since their technical inspection has exceeded one year, and in international road transport, a period longer than six months,

- i) does not have a properly marked vehicle,

- j) does not allow the exercise of state professional supervision and supreme state supervision.

(2) The transport authority or the Ministry of Transport, in case of a violation of this Act, shall impose a fine of up to CZK 500,000 on a carrier who:

- a) transports dangerous goods that are prohibited from being transported by road,

- b) transports dangerous goods without a permit prescribed by this Act,

- c) fails to comply with the conditions stipulated for the transport of dangerous goods,

- d) operates scheduled passenger transport without a license.

p36.text:
(1) Proceedings for imposing a fine may be initiated within one year from the day the transport authority or the Ministry of Transport learned of the violation of the stated obligations, but no later than five years from the day the violation occurred.

(2) When determining the amount of the fine, the seriousness, significance, and duration of the unlawful act and the extent of the damage caused shall be taken into account.

(3) Fines imposed under this Act are revenue of the state budget. If a fine is imposed by a transport authority that is not part of a district office, the fine is revenue of the municipality.

p37.text:
(1) The authorities of the Police of the Czech Republic, within the scope of supervision over the safety and fluidity of road traffic according to a special regulation^9), control whether the vehicle in operation is equipped with the documents prescribed by this Act and whether the vehicle in operation is marked or otherwise equipped in the prescribed manner.

(2) The authorities of state professional supervision over occupational safety, within the scope of supervision over occupational safety according to a special regulation^10), control the fulfillment of conditions stipulated by this Act, if they relate to occupational safety.

(3) The authorities according to paragraphs 1 and 2 are obliged to report detected violations to the locally competent transport authority.

p38.text:
(1) Customs authorities at state borders shall inspect whether vehicle crews crossing the borders possess the prescribed documents authorising international road transport, documents on compliance with the prescribed driving and rest periods, whether they are not foreign carriers prohibited by the Ministry of Transport from operating road transport in the territory of the Czech Republic, and whether a domestic carrier uses a vehicle whose technical condition was found satisfactory during a technical inspection, from which the period stipulated by this Act has not elapsed.

(2) For vehicles transporting dangerous goods, customs authorities at state borders shall inspect whether the vehicle crew is, in cases stipulated by an international agreement on the transport of dangerous goods or by this Act, equipped with instructions drawn up in the languages stipulated by the international agreement, whether they possess a certificate of vehicle approval for the transport of dangerous goods and of crew competence according to a special regulation, whether the consignment note is filled in the prescribed manner, and whether vehicles transporting dangerous goods are marked and equipped in the prescribed manner.

(3) In the event of non-compliance with the stipulated conditions, customs authorities are authorised to refuse entry into or exit from the Czech Republic for the vehicle.

(4) Customs authorities may, in justified cases, hand over substitute entry permits issued by the Ministry of Transport to foreign carriers.

p39.heading: Proceedings in matters regulated by this Act
p39.text: Proceedings in matters regulated by this Act shall be conducted in accordance with general regulations on administrative proceedings, unless individual provisions of this Act stipulate otherwise.

p40.text:
(1) Carriers operating road transport (public regular transport, international bus transport, and international freight transport) on the basis of a concession under the Trade Licensing Act are obliged to submit a certificate of professional competence for operating transport to the relevant trade licensing office within three years from the effective date of this Act. If carriers fail to submit the certificate within this period, the issued concession shall cease to be valid upon the expiry of this period.

(2) Carriers operating regular passenger transport on the basis of a concession are obliged to bring their activities into compliance with this Act and to apply for a licence (§ 10) within one year from the effective date of this Act. Until the decision of the transport authority, or, in the case of international regular transport, the Ministry of Transport, these carriers shall be considered as carriers to whom a licence has been granted under this Act.

p41.heading: Authorising Provisions
p41.text: The Ministry of Transport shall issue legal regulations for the implementation of § 3 (2), § 6 (4), § 7 (3), § 9 (1), § 17 (4), § 21 (3), § 22 (3), § 23 (1), § 30 (2).

p42.heading: Repealing Provisions
p42.text: Act No. 68/1979 Sb., on Road Transport and Domestic Forwarding, as amended by Act No. 118/1990 Sb., is hereby repealed.

p43.text:
This Act shall enter into force on 1 August 1994.

**Uhde m.p.**

**Havel m.p.**

**Klaus m.p.**

^1) Act No. 455/1991 Sb., on Trade Licensing (Trade Licensing Act), as amended. Act No. 513/1991 Sb., Commercial Code, as amended.

^2) § 1 and § 5 (4) of Czech National Council Act No. 425/1990 Sb., on District Offices, the Adjustment of their Competence and on Certain Other Related Measures, as amended (full wording No. 403/1992 Sb.) § 13 (1) of Czech National Council Act No. 418/1990 Sb., on the Capital City of Prague, as amended (full wording No. 90/1993 Sb.)

^3) § 58 (5) of Act No. 367/1990 Sb., on Municipalities, as amended.

^4) Decree of the Czech Office for Occupational Safety No. 213/1991 Sb., on Occupational Safety and Technical Equipment in the Operation, Maintenance and Repair of Vehicles. § 8, 9, 11, 12 of the Ministry of Interior's instruction ref. no. SD/33-1908/75 on determining the working hours of driving personnel in road and urban transport, registered in part 33/1975 Sb., as amended by the Ministry of Interior's instruction ref. no. SD/33-2831/77, registered in part 2/1978 Sb.

^5) Act No. 455/1991 Sb., as amended.

^6) Act No. 135/1961 Sb., on Roads (Road Act), as amended.

^7) European Agreement concerning the International Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road - ADR (Geneva, 1957) promulgated in the Collection of Laws under No. 64/1987.

^8) Czech National Council Act No. 61/1988 Sb., on Mining Activities, Explosives and State Mining Administration, as amended. Act No. 238/1991 Sb., on Waste. Government Decree of the Czech Socialist Republic No. 192/1988 Sb., on Poisons and Other Substances Harmful to Health, as amended.

^9) Czech National Council Act No. 283/1991 Sb., on the Police of the Czech Republic, as amended.

^10) Act No. 174/1968 Sb., on State Expert Supervision of Occupational Safety, as amended (full wording No. 396/1992 Sb.).
